A study space on AI agents.

Notes, experiments and open source code to understand how to build AI agents that actually reason. A personal laboratory open to anyone who wants to learn together.

Open SourceMIT LicensePersonal lab
Discover the book Go to the blog

What I do here

📖

Study

Deep dives on AI agents, Knowledge Graphs, hybrid retrieval and emerging protocols. What I learn I write down, what I write down I publish.

🧪

Experimentation

Open source prototypes to test architectures, patterns and ideas. Runnable code, not just theory.

📚

Dissemination

Technical books, articles and tutorials that make enterprise-grade techniques accessible to self-taught learners.

🌱

Community

An open space: anyone who wants to read, discuss or contribute is welcome. No roles, no memberships.

Publications

Technical books for those who learn by doing

April 2026

AI that Reasons

Chatbots reply. Systems that reason decide. Over 500 pages of runnable Python code to build enterprise Knowledge Graphs, hybrid Graph-RAG pipelines, MCP servers and agent crews orchestrated with Microsoft Agent Framework. From theory to deployment, no shortcuts.

500+ full-color pages Paperback · Hardcover · Kindle
PythonNeo4jRedisGraph-RAGMCPAgent Framework
Coming soon on Amazon

Stay in touch

If you're interested in what I publish, there are a few simple ways to follow the work: the code is on GitHub, articles land on the blog, and the occasional update goes on LinkedIn.

GitHub LinkedIn Newsletter

Write to me: gzileni@agentengineering.it

Projects

Public project linked to the book "AI that Reasons". Other repositories are under review and will be published progressively.

🧠 In Development

knowledge-graph

Companion project of the book "AI that Reasons". Knowledge graph with ingestion pipelines, hybrid retrieval (vector + graph), web UI and multi-agent orchestration.

PythonFastAPILangGraphNeo4jRedisFastMCPNext.js 15
